title: Optional NDJSON streaming response for /api/tracks
description: When the client sends Accept: application/x-ndjson, stream rows incrementally so the UI can render as data arrives. Default JSON-list response preserved for autocue-qa Playwright back-compat.
acceptanceCriteria: Accept: application/json -> existing JSON list response (default).
Accept: application/x-ndjson -> StreamingResponse with one JSON object per line.
Frontend can render first ~50 cards within 200ms of request start (perceived faster paint).
ETag (TASK-023) is still emitted for the NDJSON variant; 304 path returns empty body.

steps.details: if 'application/x-ndjson' in request.headers.get('accept', ''): return ndjson_response() else return list_response().
async def stream(): for item in payload: yield (json.dumps(item) + '\n').encode('utf-8'). Return StreamingResponse(stream(), media_type='application/x-ndjson').

docs/index.html: extend _consumeSSE pattern (the existing fetch + ReadableStream reader) to parse NDJSON. Each newline = one TrackItem. Push into parsedTracks and signal AppState 'tracks' for incremental render.

tests/test_serve_routes.py: request Accept: application/x-ndjson; assert response.headers['content-type'] starts with 'application/x-ndjson'; parse lines; assert track count matches. Vitest: stub the streaming fetch and assert incremental renders fire.

technicalNotes:
Per CLAUDE.md SSE consumption: 'New SSE-driven UI should reuse _consumeSSE rather than re-inlining the reader loop.' NDJSON is similar to SSE but framed by '
' not 'data: ... 

'. Share the underlying reader; just swap the framer.
Don't break the GZipMiddleware path - NDJSON should still compress (minimum_size=1000 will skip small responses, but full library payloads are huge).
